novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship; the comparison omits optimal-recovery/convex-function-class smoothness bounds such as ArmstrongKolesar2018, and the current kernel reads as a field-level DID specialization of C^{1,1} extension geometry unless tied to a named open no-QUG/minimax frontier.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add Armstrong and Kolesár (2018) and Donoho-style optimal-recovery literature, then state exactly why the clipped-arc identified set is not just an optimal-recovery support functional with a DID relabeling.

Spell the full multi-gap optimization problem as a finite program with dimension, variables, common-intercept constraints, boundary variables, and one objective for each endpoint; the current interior-gap block is concrete but the global program is still schematic.

To meet the flagship floor, upgrade Conjecture 1 into a strict extension of a named published open regime: e.g. a sharp no-QUG branch for DCDHK-style workflows with a computable observable threshold, or a minimax-optimal smoothness-bound frontier that existing optimal-inference papers do not cover.
Add an explicit nondegenerate witness family for U_strict with endpoint values and derivative traces showing 0<lower<upper<B under rho-open perturbations.

recommended_next_step: Revise the literature comparison and flagship claim: either embrace a field-tier support-gap theorem or strengthen Conjecture 1 into a named open-regime/minimax frontier that clears the flagship target.
